label: JK BMS Heater Control
info:
Solar-surplus water-heater control driven by the JK BMS feed.

State machine: idle → probe → run → backoff.

* idle    — heater off, waiting for conditions
* probe   — heater on for `probe_seconds` to test if surplus is real
* run     — heater on, conditions still met
* backoff — surplus disappeared; heater off, lockout for `backoff_minutes`

Guards (all must hold to leave idle):
  • SOC ≥ input_number.heater_soc_floor
  • Sun elevation ≥ input_number.heater_sun_elevation_min
  • BMS reports charging with charging_power > 0
  • BMS online

Drop-out from probe / run:
  • Sustained battery discharge > input_number.heater_discharge_threshold_w → backoff
  • Conditions otherwise lost → idle

Output: writes input_boolean.heater_request (on/off) and input_text.heater_state
(idle/probe/run/backoff). Wire your real heater relay to follow heater_request
later — flow stays unchanged.

Server reference is intentionally blank — Node-RED will prompt you to select
your existing Home Assistant server on import.

name: Heater FSM
